headline: Carbice and Noctua Partner to Bring Advanced Thermal Pad Technology to DIY PC Builders
summary: Carbice, a U.S.-based developer and supplier of next-generation carbon nanotube thermal interface materials (TIMs) and high-performance cooling solutions, today announced a long-term strategic partnership with Noctua, the Austrian leader in premium quiet PC cooling. Under the agreement, Noctua will exclusively distribute Carbice® IP90 thermal pads as a no-maintenance thermal paste alternative for DIY PC builders, and the two companies will collaborate on future product development to bring innov

headline: Nvidia Bets $6.5B on Photonics to Solve AI’s Next Big Bottleneck
summary: Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has committed at least $6.5 billion over the past three months to photonics companies, as it looks for ways to remove one of the biggest bottlenecks in AI infrastructure, according to CNBC. For context, photonics uses light to move data instead of electricity, which can make data transfer faster and more energy efficient. That matters because today’s AI systems depend on massive amounts of data moving between GPUs, memory, networking chips, servers, and data centers. Clai

headline: Nvidia Just Derailed the Chip Rally. Intel, AMD Are Falling Sharply.
summary: Nvidia  has been on the defensive against  Intel  and  Advanced Micro Devices  in recent months when it comes to artificial-intelligence processors.  Nvidia late on Sunday unveiled the RTX Spark, which it described as “the most efficient PC chip ever built,” designed for personal devices to run AI agents—programs that can act autonomously.  The move is an effort to disrupt Intel and AMD’s dominance of the PC chip market, with Nvidia using technology licensed from processor-design firm  Arm Holding  It will also be competing with  Qualcomm  which has made a push into the PC market with a range of AI chips.

The move is an effort to disrupt Intel and AMD’s dominance of the PC chip market, with Nvidia using technology licensed from processor-design firm  Arm Holding  It will also be competing with  Qualcomm  which has made a push into the PC market with a range of AI chips.

headline: Wall Street futures gain as AI advances overshadow US-Iran tensions
summary: U.S. stock index futures climbed on Monday, starting June on a firm footing, as the latest AI push from Nvidia and Microsoft ‌lifted shares, even as uncertainty over the Middle East war lingered.  Nvidia rose 1.6% ‌in premarket trading after the world's most valuable company unveiled a new chip that brings AI capabilities to ​laptops and desktops, set for release this fall.  The chip is a result of a three-year partnership with Microsoft to "reinvent the PC" for the AI era, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said.

content.summary: U.S. stock index futures climbed on Monday, starting June on a firm footing, as the latest AI push from Nvidia and Microsoft ‌lifted shares, even as uncertainty over the Middle East war lingered.  Nvidia rose 1.6% ‌in premarket trading after the world's most valuable company unveiled a new chip that brings AI capabilities to ​laptops and desktops, set for release this fall.  The chip is a result of a three-year partnership with Microsoft to "reinvent the PC" for the AI era, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said.

headline: U.S. to close loophole allowing advanced chips to Chinese firms, Reuters says
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce has moved to close a potential loophole it created that may have allowed companies to export Nvidia’s (NVDA) Rubin and Blackwell processors as well as AMD‘s (AMD) MI350 to Chinese entities located outside of China, Karen Freifeld of Reuters reports. This guidance suggests these AI chips may have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms for almost a year.Claim 55% Off TipRanksUnlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, fr

headline: Why Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Is Up 10.4% After Launching 2nm EPYC Venice Production
summary: In May 2026, Advanced Micro Devices said its next‑generation EPYC “Venice” data center CPUs had entered production on TSMC’s advanced 2nm process in Taiwan, with plans to expand manufacturing to TSMC’s Arizona facility and extend 2nm across its future EPYC roadmap. This early move onto 2nm for high‑performance computing underpins AMD’s push to be a core supplier for AI infrastructure, tying together cutting‑edge process technology, global manufacturing capacity, and growing AI‑oriented CPU...
